input: Create or improve a GitHub Copilot custom agent for this repo so it routes work through real tools, real skills, and real subagents instead of stale names. Keep the top-level agent contract lean and move standards plus deterministic checks into deeper assets.
reference: A strong response validates the live repo and session surface before naming tools or agents, separates frontmatter, routing, handoffs, and minimization concerns, and extracts deterministic checks to scripts with standards in references.
criteria: Uses real repo or session capabilities instead of inventing tools or agents
Separates triggering, routing, handoffs, and minimization into distinct concerns
Keeps the top-level contract lean by pushing standards and mechanical checks deeper

input: Improve a custom agent that still mentions old skill helper names. I want the contract rewritten so it survives future renames by checking the live inventory first and only then naming concrete helpers.
reference: A strong response prioritizes discovery-first routing, explains that live session inventory overrides stale repo snapshots, and updates eval coverage so invented helper names regress visibly.
criteria: Explains how to handle repo or session drift
Adds or recommends eval coverage for stale helper regressions

input: I need a custom agent that can orchestrate research, judging, and training tasks in this workspace without stealing work from specialists. Write guidance that makes the handoff boundaries explicit.
reference: A strong response defines bounded ownership, names the correct task-specific owners only after verifying them, and explains when the orchestrator should keep the work instead of handing off.
criteria: Defines bounded ownership and handoff rules
Verifies the available specialist agents before hardcoding names
Explains when not to hand off
